blocks.text: DTC P1549: Charging device voltage high

The LIN-connected A.C. generator converts a portion of the engine output into electric power via the drive belt, receives instructions from the PGM-FI ECU via LIN on the voltage to be generated, and generates power accordingly The only connection terminals between the A.C. generator and the vehicle side are the +B terminal (output terminal) and LIN communication terminal (1P connector ), and the enclosure of the A.C. generator is the GND. The power supply for regulator operation is connected internally using the +B terminal. 12.5V-14.5V, the target generator voltage of the A.C. generator, is indicated by the PGM-FI ECU via LIN. If the FI MAIN RLY OUT terminal voltage remains above the specified value for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: off
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed

blocks.text: When the FI MAIN RLY OUT terminal voltage of the PGM-FI ECU remains at 16.0 V or higher for 60 seconds or longer.
If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory without the PGM-FI warning light being turned on.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
